sourceTitle: Connect a custom domain with Notion Sites

questions.question: Who is eligible to connect a custom domain with Notion Sites?

options.text: Workspace owners on paid plans
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that this feature is only available on paid plans for workspace owners.

options.text: Free workspace users
options.explanation: Custom domains are a paid feature reserved for workspace owners on paid plans.

options.text: Anyone with an internet connection
options.explanation: This feature is restricted to specific paid workspace tiers.

options.text: Administrators of the DNS provider
options.explanation: The user themselves must own the workspace plan to connect the domain.

questions.question: What is the monthly cost for the custom domain add-on when billing annually?

options.explanation: $10 is the standard monthly rate, but the annual billing unlocks a discount.
The article notes that annual billing unlocks a discounted rate of an additional $8 per month.
$12 is not mentioned as a pricing tier in the article.
$5 is not mentioned as a pricing tier for the add-on.

questions.question: When adding a custom domain, which of the following is a mandatory requirement?

options.text: The domain must be purchased from Notion
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that Notion does not sell custom domains; you must already own one from a DNS provider.

options.text: You must include a subdomain (e.g., www)
options.explanation: The instructions state that when entering your domain, you must include a subdomain.

options.text: The domain must end in .com
options.explanation: Any valid domain extension is allowed, provided it has a subdomain.

options.text: The domain must be verified before purchase
options.explanation: Verification happens during the setup process after entering the domain.

questions.question: What is the correct CNAME target value to use when setting up your DNS record?

options.text: yourdomain.com
options.explanation: This is your own domain, not the target provided by Notion.

options.text: external.notion.site
options.explanation: The article instructs you to set the CNAME target to 'external.notion.site', noting the period at the end.

options.text: www.notion.site
options.explanation: The correct target is 'external.notion.site', not 'www.notion.site'.

options.text: notion.site
options.explanation: The specific target required is 'external.notion.site'.

questions.question: According to the article, if you want to use your root domain (e.g., mydomain.com) with Notion Sites, what is the recommended method?

options.text: Point the root domain directly to external.notion.site
options.explanation: Notion does not support using the root domain directly; a subdomain is required.

options.text: Set up a subdomain (like www) and set up a redirect with your DNS provider
options.explanation: The article recommends setting up a subdomain (typically www) and configuring a redirect for your root domain.

options.text: Do not use the root domain at all
options.explanation: You can use the root domain, but only by redirecting it to a configured subdomain.

options.text: Contact Notion support to enable root domain support
options.explanation: This is not mentioned as a solution; the recommended method is using a subdomain and redirect.

questions.question: What happens to your Notion Sites when you delete a custom domain?

options.text: They are immediately unpublished and inaccessible
options.explanation: Sites remain active and accessible until the end of your current billing cycle.

options.text: They are archived and saved for 30 days
options.explanation: Archiving is not mentioned; the sites remain live during the billing period.

options.text: They remain active until the end of your current billing cycle
options.explanation: The article notes that you can still use the domain until the billing cycle ends.

options.text: They are deleted permanently
options.explanation: The sites themselves are not deleted, only the domain association is severed.

questions.question:: In the example of an incorrect DNS configuration provided in the article, what was the specific error with the TXT record?

options.text: The record name was set to `notes` but should be `_notion-dcv.notes`
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that the name should be `_notion-dcv.notes`, not `notes`.

options.text: The record value was set to `notes` but should be the unique code
options.explanation: While the value should be the unique code, the primary error highlighted in the text is the incorrect name.

options.text: The record type was set to CNAME instead of TXT
options.explanation: The text describes the error within a TXT record configuration.

options.text: There was no error; the configuration was correct
options.explanation: The section is titled 'Examples of incorrect DNS configurations'.

questions.question: How does the billing cadence for the custom domain add-on match your main Notion billing?

options.text: It is always annual regardless of your plan
options.explanation: The billing cadence must match your billing for Notion, whether that is monthly or annual.

options.text: It must be paid in full upfront
options.explanation: The billing cadence matches the existing plan (monthly/annual), not a lump sum payment.

options.text: It must be monthly
options.explanation: If you pay for Notion annually, the add-on is also billed annually.

options.text: It matches your billing for Notion
options.explanation: The text states that billing for the custom domain add-on must match your billing for Notion.

questions.question: When setting up DNS records in Cloudflare, what specific step must be taken regarding the Proxy status?

options.text: Turn Proxy status to 'On'
options.explanation: The article specifies that you must toggle Proxy status to 'Off' for the DNS records.

options.text: Ensure the DNS records are not saved
options.explanation: You must save the records after adding them.

options.text: Set the Proxy status to 'Gray Cloud'
options.explanation: The correct action is toggling it 'Off', not setting it to a gray cloud state.

options.text: Turn Proxy status to 'On' to ensure security
options.explanation: Cloudflare instructions explicitly state to make sure Proxy status is toggled off.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of custom domains you can purchase add-ons for within a single workspace?

options.text: Unlimited
options.explanation: There is a specific limit mentioned in the article.
The article states you can do this for up to 25 custom domains.
The limit is 25, not 10.
The limit is 25, not 5.

questions.question: When adding a custom domain to an existing paid plan mid-cycle, how is the initial charge calculated?

options.text: It is a full month's charge
options.explanation: The charge is prorated for the remaining time in the cycle.

options.text: It is doubled
options.explanation: There is no mention of doubling the charge.

options.text: It is prorated for the remaining time left on the current billing cycle
options.explanation: The article notes you will pay a prorated amount for the remaining time.

options.text: It is free for the first month
options.explanation: You are charged for the add-on, though prorated for the remaining time.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a step required when adding a CNAME and TXT record in general DNS provider instructions?

options.text: Add the CNAME and TXT DNS record
options.explanation: Adding the records is the first step in all provider instructions.

options.text: Select Save All Records or Save Changes
options.explanation: Saving the changes is the final step in all provider instructions.

options.text: Set the subdomain to the root domain
options.explanation: The article advises against using the root domain directly; you must use a subdomain. This is not a standard step for adding records.

options.text: Confirm the records have been successfully added
options.explanation: Confirming successful addition is the step listed after saving in all provider instructions.

questions.question: Can you remove Notion branding from your site when using a custom domain?

options.text: No, branding cannot be removed
options.explanation: You have the option to remove branding.

options.text: Yes, but only for the root domain
options.explanation: You can remove branding for the custom domain you are using.

options.text: Yes, for any custom domain you connect
options.explanation: The article states you have the option to remove any Notion branding for that custom domain.

options.text: Only if you pay an additional fee
options.explanation: Removing branding is included as an option after connecting the domain.
